title: Icacorea angustifolia
intro: Icacorea angustifolia, also known as Pata de Vaca, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. The health benefits of Icacorea angustifolia include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Icacorea angustifolia is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, often in combination with other plants, to treat various health conditions, including diabetes and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Icacorea angustifolia is a species within the flowering plant family Icacinaceae. The Icacinaceae family is part of the order Icacinales. This order is further classified under the clade asterids, which is a subgroup within the larger clade of angiosperms. The kingdom of plants to which Icacorea angustifolia belongs is Plantae. Further classification places Icacorea angustifolia within the larger group of eudicots.
